meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Master Deployment: From Local Code to Live Production App
content.seo_description: Stop building apps that never launch. Learn the professional deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to take your projects from local to live.
content.answer_block: Breaking out of 'tutorial hell' requires moving from local scripts to production-ready deployments. By integrating Antigravity for infrastructure orchestration and Claude Code for automated environment configuration, you bridge the gap between a prototype and a live, scalable application, ensuring your portfolio projects actually work for real users.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect logic, debugging local variables, and polishing your UI. Yet, your app exists only on your machine, a ghost in the shell that no one else can see. The chasm between 'it works on my computer' and 'it works for the world' is where most aspiring developers lose their momentum.

Today, we stop building toys and start shipping products. We are going to master the deployment workflow—the silent engine of every senior engineer. By leveraging Antigravity for automated infrastructure and Claude Code for intelligent CLI assistance, you will transform your local codebase into a live, professional web presence.

steps.title: Environment Audit
steps.description: Before deploying, ensure your dependencies are locked. We use Claude Code to scan for missing configuration files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit my project root for missing production-ready configuration files like docker-compose.yml or cloud-config.yaml.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'analyze current directory for deployment readiness and generate missing config files'

steps.title: Infrastructure Orchestration
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your cloud requirements without writing complex Terraform or YAML manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable infrastructure blueprint for a Node.js and PostgreSQL app, including auto-scaling and basic CI/CD hooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'apply the infrastructure blueprint provided by Antigravity to my project'

steps.title: Automated Environment Variables
steps.description: Hardcoded secrets are a security nightmare. We automate the injection of environment variables securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure template for environment variables that integrates with cloud secrets managers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'refactor config.js to pull all secrets from process.env and generate a .env.example file'

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Setup
steps.description: Automate your testing and deployment so every 'git push' triggers a fresh build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests before deploying to the cloud provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'initialize a github actions workflow file for automated testing and deployment'

steps.title: Health Check & Live Launch
steps.description: Verify the deployment with automated smoke tests before sharing your link.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a health-check script that pings the production endpoint and verifies database connectivity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'deploy the application and run the health check script to verify production status'

pro_tips: Always use a 'staging' environment before pushing to production; it prevents 90% of deployment-day heartaches.
Implement structured logging from day one so you aren't flying blind when an error occurs in the cloud.
Automate your database migrations within the CI/CD pipeline to ensure schema parity across environments.
Use ephemeral environments for every pull request to test features in isolation before merging to main.
Always set up budget alerts in your cloud provider immediately after deployment to avoid unexpected billing spikes.
content.next_steps: Once your first app is live, focus on setting up monitoring tools like Sentry or Datadog to track user errors and performance metrics in real-time.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow used by top-tier engineers to move from local experimentation to professional deployment. Don't let your code gather dust on your hard drive—put it in the hands of users and build your reputation. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
